agent: Wave N3 — live broadcast Demo A (CCTP → Arc swap)

scriptUsed.note: Script not on this branch's base (feat/wk1m4-fill-runbook-tbds). Broadcast was executed against PR #87's head with two N3 in-flight patches (Pyth-refresh-pre-swap step + PoolSwapTest.swap encoding) that did NOT land in this PR — they're proposed for a separate script-fix PR. The captured CCTP tx hashes are immutable on-chain artefacts regardless of script location.

keeper.note: Used as keeper, maker, and taker — the configured DEMO_TAKER + DEMO_MAKER EOAs have zero Fuji USDC + AVAX, so the broadcast collapsed all three roles onto KEEPER for the live run.

arc.approvePoolManagerNote: Approved PoolManager (0x3FA22b…3B34) to spend 200000 USDC raw from keeper, so PoolSwapTest's settle path inside unlockCallback can pull input.
arc.swapStatus: 0x0 (REVERTED — broadcast on-chain via cast send --gas-limit 800000 to capture the precise revert reason as an on-chain artefact)
arc.swapRevertDecoded: WrappedError(FxSwapHook=0xC6F894…0aC8, beforeSwap=0x575e24b4, inner=WrappedError(USDC=0x3600…, transfer=0xa9059cbb, reason=Error('ERC20: transfer amount exceeds balance')))
arc.swapAmountSpecified: -10000 (exact-input 0.01 USDC, reduced from 0.1 to lower failure cost)

outcome.cctpEndToEnd: PROVED LIVE. Fuji burn → Iris attest (5.9s) → Arc mint succeeded. 0.1 USDC moved from Fuji custody to Arc keeper balance, confirmed by ERC20 balance delta on Arc (22.124 → 22.215). Settles the load-bearing 'CCTP V2 attestation routed under the hook' claim from §2 of the Hookathon brief.
outcome.pythOracleWarmable: PROVED LIVE. FxOracle.getMidWithUpdatePyth(USDC, EURC, [hermesVAA]) succeeded and returned a fresh mid (0.860375 EURC/USDC, ~EUR/USD 1.16). This closes M4-BLOCK-2 (StalePrice 0x19abf40e) as long as the refresh runs in the same block as the swap.
outcome.v4SwapLeg: STILL BLOCKED — newly precise root cause. PoolSwapTest (Wave N2a pin at 0x60004B08…11Fa) is architecturally incompatible with FxSwapHook's PMM pattern: PoolSwapTest settles input AFTER manager.swap returns (standard v4 LP flow), but FxSwapHook calls inputCurrency.take(POOL_MANAGER, ...) INSIDE beforeSwap (line 731 of FxSwapHook.sol), which requires the PoolManager to already hold the input currency. Verified on-chain by the reverted swap tx 0xde83acb7…62f6 — revert path: PoolSwapTest.swap → manager.unlock → unlockCallback → manager.swap → FxSwapHook.beforeSwap → manager.take(USDC) → USDC.transfer(hook) reverts with 'ERC20: transfer amount exceeds balance' (manager holds 0 USDC at that point). The correct router shape ships in fx-telarana at `contracts/test/utils/FxV4RouterHarness.sol` — it does `_settleFrom(input, sender, amountIn)` BEFORE calling `manager.swap`, which seeds PoolManager with input before the hook tries to take it. M3 needs to deploy FxV4RouterHarness (or an equivalent settle-before-swap shim) on Arc Testnet and re-pin V4_SWAP_ROUTER_5042002.
outcome.loadBearingClaim: Real-Time FX Swap Pool Using CCTP — TWO OF THREE legs proven live (CCTP attestation routed end-to-end; Pyth oracle refresh inline before the swap block). The third leg (v4 hook-driven swap settlement) IS one router-deploy away — the FxSwapHook PMM contract itself is healthy (probe-arc-fx-swap-hook ok, reserves 1 USDC + 0.9 EURC, quote-rate would be 0.917 EURC per USDC). Honesty note: Demo A is not yet a single-tx end-to-end USDC→EURC swap; it's a two-step (CCTP-deliver-USDC THEN hook-swap-USDC→EURC) flow where step 2 needs the right router pinned.

M4-BLOCK-1.name: V4_SWAP_TEST_ROUTER not deployed
M4-BLOCK-1.newPrecisionFromN3: Wave N2a deployed canonical v4-core PoolSwapTest (0x60004B08…11Fa). PoolSwapTest IS deployed and IS callable (verified — manager() returns 0x3FA22b…, swap selector 0x2229d0b4 present), but it is NOT the right router shape for FxSwapHook's settle-inside-beforeSwap PMM pattern. The closure path is to deploy FxV4RouterHarness from fx-telarana/contracts/test/utils/FxV4RouterHarness.sol.
M4-BLOCK-1.fixOwner: Wave M3 (or a new wave N4) — re-pin V4_SWAP_ROUTER_5042002 to an FxV4RouterHarness deployment.

M4-BLOCK-2.name: FxOracle.getMid(USDC,EURC) reverts StalePrice()
M4-BLOCK-2.closureMechanism: Wave N3 demonstrated inline Pyth refresh via FxOracle.getMidWithUpdatePyth using Hermes VAA in the same tx. Tx 0x7b1cb2f4…14e5c (and the second warm 0x097eea5c…1fa81). N2b's keep-warm daemon is the production-time closure; this wave's inline refresh is the per-broadcast workaround that keeps the swap block fresh.

M4-BLOCK-3.name: Circle Gateway attestation env not provisioned
M4-BLOCK-3.note: Demo B (Gateway) only — out of scope for N3. Owned by N2c.
